```{r}
# ============================================================
# Comparative Model Analysis — Best Specifications
# French Motor Insurance (freMTPL2freq)
# All models fitted on 80/20 train/test split (seed = 123)
# No cross-validataion — hyperparameters fixed to best configs
# Outputs: results_df ready for ggplot graphing
# ============================================================

library(gbm)       # GBM
library(xgboost)   # XGBoost
library(mboost)    # GLMBoost
library(dplyr)
library(mgcv)      # GAM

# ============================================================
# 0. LOAD & PREPARE data
# ============================================================
data <- read.csv("C:/Users/matth/OneDrive/Desktop/Books/Thesis/Data/freMTPL2freq.csv", header = TRUE)

data$ClaimNb  <- pmin(data$ClaimNb, 4)
data$VehGas   <- factor(data$VehGas)
data$VehBrand <- factor(data$VehBrand)
data$Region   <- factor(data$Region)
data$Area     <- factor(data$Area)
data$VehPower <- as.numeric(data$VehPower)

set.seed(123)
n        <- nrow(data)
train_id <- sample(seq_len(n), size = floor(0.8 * n))
train    <- data[ train_id, ]
test     <- data[-train_id, ]

# ============================================================
# Helper functions
# ============================================================
poisson_deviance <- function(y, mu) {
  terms <- ifelse(y == 0, 0, y * log(y / mu)) - (y - mu)
  2 * sum(terms)
}

rmse <- function(y, yhat) sqrt(mean((y - yhat)^2))

results <- list()

# ============================================================
# 1. POISSON GLM
#    All predictors except Density (dropped by stepwise AIC)
# ============================================================
cat("Fitting Poisson GLM...\n")

glm_fit <- glm(
  ClaimNb ~ VehPower + VehAge + DrivAge + BonusMalus +
            VehBrand + VehGas + Area + Region +
            offset(log(Exposure)),
  data   = train,
  family = poisson(link = "log")
)

glm_pred <- predict(glm_fit, newdata = test, type = "response")

results[["Poisson GLM"]] <- data.frame(
  Model         = "Poisson GLM",
  Test_Deviance = poisson_deviance(test$ClaimNb, glm_pred),
  Test_RMSE     = rmse(test$ClaimNb, glm_pred)
)
cat("  Done. Test deviance:", results[["Poisson GLM"]]$Test_Deviance, "\n")

# ============================================================
# 2. POISSON GAM — mB_cr
#    s(VehPower) + s(DrivAge) + s(BonusMalus), bs="cr", REML
#    + all categorical predictors
# ============================================================
cat("Fitting GAM (mB_cr)...\n")

gam_fit <- gam(
  ClaimNb ~ s(VehPower, bs = "cr") +
            s(DrivAge,  bs = "cr") +
            s(BonusMalus, bs = "cr") +
            VehBrand + VehGas + Area + Region +
            offset(log(Exposure)),
  data   = train,
  family = poisson(link = "log"),
  method = "REML"
)

gam_pred <- predict(gam_fit, newdata = test, type = "response")

results[["GAM"]] <- data.frame(
  Model         = "GAM (mB_cr)",
  Test_Deviance = poisson_deviance(test$ClaimNb, gam_pred),
  Test_RMSE     = rmse(test$ClaimNb, gam_pred)
)
cat("  Done. Test deviance:", results[["GAM"]]$Test_Deviance, "\n")

# ============================================================
# 3. GBM — Interaction model (best of 5 configs)
#    depth=6, lr=0.020, bag=0.5, min_obs=50, M*=3991
#    predict.gbm does NOT add offset — add log(Exposure) manually
# ============================================================
cat("Fitting GBM (Interaction, M*=3991)...\n")

set.seed(123)
gbm_fit <- gbm(
  ClaimNb ~ VehPower + VehAge + DrivAge + BonusMalus +
            VehBrand + VehGas + Area + Region + Density +
            offset(log(Exposure)),
  data              = train,
  distribution      = "poisson",
  n.trees           = 3991,
  interaction.depth = 6,
  shrinkage         = 0.020,
  bag.fraction      = 0.5,
  n.minobsinnode    = 50,
  verbose           = FALSE
)

# predict() returns log-rate (F(x) only, no offset) — add log(Exposure) manually
gbm_pred_log <- predict(gbm_fit, newdata = test, n.trees = 3991)
gbm_pred     <- exp(gbm_pred_log + log(test$Exposure))

results[["GBM"]] <- data.frame(
  Model         = "GBM (Interaction)",
  Test_Deviance = poisson_deviance(test$ClaimNb, gbm_pred),
  Test_RMSE     = rmse(test$ClaimNb, gbm_pred)
)
cat("  Done. Test deviance:", results[["GBM"]]$Test_Deviance, "\n")

# ============================================================
# 4. XGBoost — Model 36 best config
#    eta=0.05, depth=8, min_child=1, sub=0.7, colsub=0.7,
#    gamma=0, M*=490
#    base_margin=log(Exposure) handles offset at DMatrix level
# ============================================================
cat("Fitting XGBoost (M*=490)...\n")

train_mat <- model.matrix(~ VehPower + VehAge + DrivAge + BonusMalus +
                            VehBrand + VehGas + Area + Region + Density - 1,
                          data = train)
test_mat  <- model.matrix(~ VehPower + VehAge + DrivAge + BonusMalus +
                            VehBrand + VehGas + Area + Region + Density - 1,
                          data = test)

dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data = train_mat, label = train$ClaimNb,
                      base_margin = log(train$Exposure))
dtest  <- xgb.DMatrix(data = test_mat,  label = test$ClaimNb,
                      base_margin = log(test$Exposure))

xgb_params <- list(
  objective        = "count:poisson",
  eval_metric      = "poisson-nloglik",
  eta              = 0.05,
  max_depth        = 8,
  min_child_weight = 1,
  subsample        = 0.7,
  colsample_bytree = 0.7,
  gamma            = 0
)

set.seed(123)
xgb_fit <- xgb.train(
  params  = xgb_params,
  data    = dtrain,
  nrounds = 490,
  verbose = 0
)

# predict() returns expected counts directly (base_margin already included)
xgb_pred <- predict(xgb_fit, dtest)

results[["XGBoost"]] <- data.frame(
  Model         = "XGBoost",
  Test_Deviance = poisson_deviance(test$ClaimNb, xgb_pred),
  Test_RMSE     = rmse(test$ClaimNb, xgb_pred)
)
cat("  Done. Test deviance:", results[["XGBoost"]]$Test_Deviance, "\n")

# ============================================================
# 5. GLMBoost — nu=0.50, mstop=4473
#    Offset entered via formula as offset(LogExp)
#    predict() returns log-rate including offset — exponentiate directly
# ============================================================
cat("Fitting GLMBoost (nu=0.50, mstop=4473)...\n")

train_glmb        <- train
test_glmb         <- test
train_glmb$LogExp <- log(train$Exposure)
test_glmb$LogExp  <- log(test$Exposure)

glmb_fit <- glmboost(
  ClaimNb ~ VehPower + VehAge + DrivAge + BonusMalus +
            VehBrand + VehGas + Area + Region + Density +
            offset(LogExp),
  data    = train_glmb,
  family  = Poisson(),
  control = boost_control(mstop = 4473, nu = 0.50, center = TRUE)
)

# predict() returns linear predictor including offset on log scale
glmb_pred_log <- predict(glmb_fit, newdata = test_glmb)
glmb_pred     <- exp(glmb_pred_log)

results[["GLMBoost"]] <- data.frame(
  Model         = "GLMBoost (nu=0.50)",
  Test_Deviance = poisson_deviance(test$ClaimNb, glmb_pred),
  Test_RMSE     = rmse(test$ClaimNb, glmb_pred)
)
cat("  Done. Test deviance:", results[["GLMBoost"]]$Test_Deviance, "\n")

# ============================================================
# 6. HYBRID GLM + GBM (Without Density)
#    Stage 1: Poisson GLM (reuses glm_fit above)
#    Stage 2: GBM correction with combined offset = log(Exposure) + eta_glm
#    depth=3, M*=5318, lr=0.010, bag=0.7, min_obs=100
#    predict.gbm does NOT add offset — add combined_offset manually
# ============================================================
cat("Fitting Hybrid GLM + GBM (Without Density, M*=5318)...\n")

# Stage 1: extract GLM log-rate (linear predictor minus exposure offset)
eta_train <- predict(glm_fit, newdata = train, type = "link") - log(train$Exposure)
eta_test  <- predict(glm_fit, newdata = test,  type = "link") - log(test$Exposure)

# Combined offset: log(Exposure) + GLM log-rate
train$combined_offset <- log(train$Exposure) + eta_train
test$combined_offset  <- log(test$Exposure)  + eta_test

# Stage 2: GBM corrects residual log-rate left by GLM
set.seed(123)
hybrid_gbm_fit <- gbm(
  ClaimNb ~ VehPower + VehAge + DrivAge + BonusMalus +
            VehBrand + VehGas + Area + Region +
            offset(combined_offset),
  data              = train,
  distribution      = "poisson",
  n.trees           = 5318,
  interaction.depth = 3,
  shrinkage         = 0.010,
  bag.fraction      = 0.7,
  n.minobsinnode    = 100,
  verbose           = FALSE
)

# predict() returns F(x) only — add combined_offset manually
hybrid_pred_log <- predict(hybrid_gbm_fit, newdata = test, n.trees = 5318)
hybrid_pred     <- exp(hybrid_pred_log + test$combined_offset)

results[["Hybrid"]] <- data.frame(
  Model         = "Hybrid GLM+GBM",
  Test_Deviance = poisson_deviance(test$ClaimNb, hybrid_pred),
  Test_RMSE     = rmse(test$ClaimNb, hybrid_pred)
)
cat("  Done. Test deviance:", results[["Hybrid"]]$Test_Deviance, "\n")

# ============================================================
# 7. COLLECT & SAVE RESULTS
# ============================================================
results_df <- bind_rows(results) %>%
  arrange(Test_Deviance) %>%
  mutate(
    Rank          = row_number(),
    Model         = factor(Model, levels = Model),
    Test_Deviance = round(Test_Deviance, 2),
    Test_RMSE     = round(Test_RMSE, 4)
  )

cat("\n========== FINAL RESULTS ==========\n")
print(results_df)

saveRDS(results_df, "results_df.rds")
cat("\nSaved results_df.rds — load with readRDS('results_df.rds') for graphing.\n")
```

```{r}
# =============================================================================
# SUPER LEARNER — CLAIM FREQUENCY MODELLING (THESIS VERSION)
# GLM + GAM + GBM + XGBOOST
# Exposure handled consistently as offset(log(Exposure))
# =============================================================================

library(caret)
library(SuperLearner)
library(mgcv)
library(gbm)
library(xgboost)
library(MASS)

# =============================================================================
# DATA
# =============================================================================

df <- read.csv(
  "C:/Users/matth/OneDrive/Desktop/Books/Thesis/Data/freMTPL2freq.csv"
)

set.seed(123)

# Ensure factors are consistent globally
df$VehBrand <- factor(df$VehBrand)
df$VehGas   <- factor(df$VehGas)
df$Area     <- factor(df$Area)
df$Region   <- factor(df$Region)

# =============================================================================
# TRAIN / TEST SPLIT
# =============================================================================

train_index <- createDataPartition(df$ClaimNb, p = 0.80, list = FALSE)

train <- df[train_index, ]
test  <- df[-train_index, ]

X_train <- train[, c("VehPower","VehAge","DrivAge","BonusMalus",
                     "VehBrand","VehGas","Area","Density","Region")]

X_test  <- test[, c("VehPower","VehAge","DrivAge","BonusMalus",
                     "VehBrand","VehGas","Area","Density","Region")]

Y_train <- train$ClaimNb
Y_test  <- test$ClaimNb

Exposure_train <- train$Exposure
Exposure_test  <- test$Exposure

# =============================================================================
# METRICS
# =============================================================================

poisson_deviance <- function(y, mu) {
  mu <- pmax(mu, 1e-15)
  2 * sum(ifelse(y == 0, mu, y * log(y / mu) - (y - mu)))
}

rmse <- function(y, pred) sqrt(mean((y - pred)^2))

# =============================================================================
# 1. GLM WRAPPER
# =============================================================================

SL.glm.poisson <- function(Y, X, newX, family, obsWeights, ...) {

  dat <- data.frame(X, ClaimNb = Y, Exposure = obsWeights)

  mod <- glm(
    ClaimNb ~ VehPower + VehAge + DrivAge + BonusMalus +
      VehBrand + VehGas + Area + Density + Region +
      offset(log(Exposure)),
    family = poisson(),
    data = dat
  )

  newdat <- data.frame(newX)
  newdat$Exposure <- 1

  pred <- predict(mod, newdata = newdat, type = "response")

  list(fit = list(model = mod), pred = pred)
}

predict.SL.glm.poisson <- function(object, newdata, ...) {
  nd <- data.frame(newdata)
  nd$Exposure <- 1
  predict(object$model, newdata = nd, type = "response")
}

# =============================================================================
# 2. GAM WRAPPER
# =============================================================================

SL.gam.poisson <- function(Y, X, newX, family, obsWeights, ...) {

  dat <- data.frame(X, ClaimNb = Y, Exposure = obsWeights)

  mod <- gam(
    ClaimNb ~
      s(VehPower, k = 8, bs = "ps") +
      s(DrivAge,  k = 8, bs = "ps") +
      s(BonusMalus, k = 8, bs = "ps") +
      VehAge + VehBrand + VehGas + Area + Region +
      offset(log(Exposure)),
    family = poisson(),
    method = "REML",
    data = dat
  )

  newdat <- data.frame(newX)
  newdat$Exposure <- 1

  pred <- predict(mod, newdata = newdat, type = "response")

  list(fit = list(model = mod), pred = pred)
}

predict.SL.gam.poisson <- function(object, newdata, ...) {
  nd <- data.frame(newdata)
  nd$Exposure <- 1
  predict(object$model, newdata = nd, type = "response")
}

# =============================================================================
# 3. GBM WRAPPER (OFFSET CORRECTED)
# =============================================================================

SL.gbm.poisson <- function(Y, X, newX, family, obsWeights, ...) {

  X <- data.frame(X)
  newX <- data.frame(newX)

  # Ensure factors are consistent
  facs <- c("VehBrand","VehGas","Area","Region")

  for (v in facs) {
    X[[v]] <- factor(X[[v]])
    newX[[v]] <- factor(newX[[v]], levels = levels(X[[v]]))
  }

  dat <- data.frame(X, ClaimNb = Y, LogExp = log(obsWeights))

  mod <- gbm(
    ClaimNb ~ . - LogExp + offset(LogExp),
    data = dat,
    distribution = "poisson",
    n.trees = 5000,
    interaction.depth = 6,
    shrinkage = 0.02,
    bag.fraction = 0.5,
    n.minobsinnode = 100,
    cv.folds = 0,
    verbose = FALSE
  )

  pred <- predict(
    mod,
    newdata = newX,
    n.trees = 3000,
    type = "response"
  )

  list(fit = list(model = mod), pred = pred)
}

predict.SL.gbm.poisson <- function(object, newdata, ...) {

  newdata <- data.frame(newdata)

  facs <- c("VehBrand","VehGas","Area","Region")

  for (v in facs) {
    newdata[[v]] <- factor(newdata[[v]])
  }

  predict(
    object$model,
    newdata = newdata,
    n.trees = 3000,
    type = "response"
  )
}

# =============================================================================
# 4. XGBOOST WRAPPER (OFFSET CORRECT)
# =============================================================================

SL.xgb.poisson <- function(Y, X, newX, family, obsWeights, ...) {

  Xmat <- model.matrix(~ . - 1, data = X)
  Xnew <- model.matrix(~ . - 1, data = newX)

  dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(Xmat, label = Y)

  setinfo(dtrain, "base_margin", log(obsWeights))

  params <- list(
    objective = "count:poisson",
    eta = 0.005,
    max_depth = 6,
    subsample = 0.7,
    colsample_bytree = 0.7,
    lambda = 1
  )

  mod <- xgb.train(
    params = params,
    data = dtrain,
    nrounds = 5000,
    verbose = 0
  )

  dtest <- xgb.DMatrix(Xnew)
  setinfo(dtest, "base_margin", rep(0, nrow(Xnew)))

  pred <- predict(mod, dtest)

  list(
    fit = list(model = mod, feature_names = colnames(Xmat)),
    pred = pred
  )
}

predict.SL.xgb.poisson <- function(object, newdata, ...) {

  Xnew <- model.matrix(~ . - 1, data = newdata)

  miss <- setdiff(object$feature_names, colnames(Xnew))

  for (m in miss) {
    Xnew <- cbind(Xnew, setNames(data.frame(0), m))
  }

  Xnew <- Xnew[, object$feature_names, drop = FALSE]

  dtest <- xgb.DMatrix(Xnew)
  setinfo(dtest, "base_margin", rep(0, nrow(Xnew)))

  predict(object$model, dtest)
}

# =============================================================================
# SUPER LEARNER FIT
# =============================================================================

sl_fit <- SuperLearner(
  Y = Y_train,
  X = X_train,
  newX = X_test,
  family = poisson(),
  SL.library = c(
    "SL.glm.poisson",
    "SL.gam.poisson",
    "SL.gbm.poisson",
    "SL.xgb.poisson"
  ),
  method = "method.NNLS",
  cvControl = list(V = 5),
  obsWeights = Exposure_train,
  verbose = TRUE
)

# =============================================================================
# EVALUATION
# =============================================================================

SL_pred <- sl_fit$SL.predict * Exposure_test

cat("\nSUPER LEARNER RESULTS\n")
cat("---------------------\n")

cat("Deviance:", poisson_deviance(Y_test, SL_pred), "\n")
cat("RMSE    :", rmse(Y_test, SL_pred), "\n\n")

print(sl_fit$coef)
print(sl_fit$cvRisk)
```

```{r}
# -----------------------------
# METRICS
# -----------------------------

poisson_deviance <- function(y, mu) {
  mu <- pmax(mu, 1e-15)
  2 * sum(ifelse(y == 0, mu, y * log(y / mu) - (y - mu)))
}

rmse <- function(y, pred) sqrt(mean((y - pred)^2))

mae <- function(y, pred) mean(abs(y - pred))

gini_index <- function(y, pred) {
  df <- data.frame(y = y, pred = pred)
  df <- df[order(-df$pred), ]
  cum_y <- cumsum(df$y)
  gini <- sum(cum_y) / sum(df$y) - (nrow(df) + 1) / 2
  gini / nrow(df)
}

# -----------------------------
# PREDICTIONS (FIXED NAMES)
# -----------------------------

individual_preds <- sl_fit$library.predict

glm_pred <- individual_preds[, "SL.glm.poisson_All"] * Exposure_test
gam_pred <- individual_preds[, "SL.gam.poisson_All"] * Exposure_test
gbm_pred <- individual_preds[, "SL.gbm.poisson_All"] * Exposure_test
xgb_pred <- individual_preds[, "SL.xgb.poisson_All"] * Exposure_test

sl_pred <- SL_pred

# -----------------------------
# COMPLEXITY SCORES
# -----------------------------

complexity_score <- c(
  GLM = 1,
  GAM = 2,
  GBM = 3,
  XGBoost = 4,
  SuperLearner = 5
)

# -----------------------------
# FINAL TABLE
# -----------------------------

results <- data.frame(
  Model = c("SuperLearner", "XGBoost", "GAM", "GLM", "GBM"),

  Deviance = c(
    poisson_deviance(Y_test, sl_pred),
    poisson_deviance(Y_test, xgb_pred),
    poisson_deviance(Y_test, gam_pred),
    poisson_deviance(Y_test, glm_pred),
    poisson_deviance(Y_test, gbm_pred)
  ),

  RMSE = c(
    rmse(Y_test, sl_pred),
    rmse(Y_test, xgb_pred),
    rmse(Y_test, gam_pred),
    rmse(Y_test, glm_pred),
    rmse(Y_test, gbm_pred)
  ),

  MAE = c(
    mae(Y_test, sl_pred),
    mae(Y_test, xgb_pred),
    mae(Y_test, gam_pred),
    mae(Y_test, glm_pred),
    mae(Y_test, gbm_pred)
  ),

  Gini = c(
    gini_index(Y_test, sl_pred),
    gini_index(Y_test, xgb_pred),
    gini_index(Y_test, gam_pred),
    gini_index(Y_test, glm_pred),
    gini_index(Y_test, gbm_pred)
  ),

  Complexity = c(
    complexity_score["SuperLearner"],
    complexity_score["XGBoost"],
    complexity_score["GAM"],
    complexity_score["GLM"],
    complexity_score["GBM"]
  )
)

# -----------------------------
# OUTPUT
# -----------------------------

print(results)
```

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}


# ============================================================
# BUILD PREDICTION DATA FRAME
# ============================================================
n_test <- 135602

glm_pred    <- glm_pred[seq_len(n_test)]
test_use    <- test[seq_len(n_test), ]
pred_df <- data.frame(
  y                = test_use$ClaimNb,
  exposure         = test_use$Exposure,
  `Poisson GLM`    = glm_pred,
  GAM              = gam_pred,
  GLMBoost         = glmb_pred,
  `Hybrid GLM+GBM` = hybrid_pred,
  GBM              = gbm_pred,
  XGBoost          = xgb_pred,
  `Super Learner`  = sl_pred,
  check.names = FALSE
)

model_names <- c("Poisson GLM","GAM","GLMBoost","Hybrid GLM+GBM","GBM","XGBoost","Super Learner")

group_map <- c(
  "Poisson GLM"    = "Statistical",
  "GAM"            = "Statistical",
  "GLMBoost"       = "Statistical",
  "Hybrid GLM+GBM" = "Hybrid",
  "GBM"            = "Machine Learning",
  "XGBoost"        = "Machine Learning",
  "Super Learner"  = "Machine Learning"
)

# ============================================================
# FIGURE 1 — PEARSON RESIDUAL PLOT (panelled by group)
# ============================================================
cat("Plotting Figure 1: Pearson Residuals...\n")

resid_long <- pred_df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(model_names), names_to = "Model", values_to = "pred") %>%
  mutate(
    resid = pearson_resid(y, pred),
    Group = group_map[Model],
    Model = factor(Model, levels = model_names)
  ) %>%
  # thin to max 30,000 rows for speed (random sample)
  slice_sample(n = min(30000, nrow(.)))

fig_resid <- ggplot(resid_long, aes(x = pred, y = resid, colour = Model)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.15, size = 0.4, shape = 16) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ Group, ncol = 3, scales = "free_x") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = model_colors) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 0.01)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1.5, 4)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Residual Structure by Model Group",
    subtitle = "Pearson residuals vs predicted claim frequency on the test set",
    x        = "Predicted claim frequency",
    y        = "Pearson residual",
    colour   = "Model",
    caption  = "Systematic patterns in the statistical group indicate structure not captured by additive or linear frameworks."
  ) +
  thesis_theme +
  guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(alpha = 1, size = 2)))

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "fig_residuals.png"), fig_resid,
       width = 12, height = 5, dpi = 300)
cat("  Saved fig_residuals.png\n")

# ============================================================
# FIGURE 2 — CALIBRATION PLOT (10 predicted-frequency bands)
# ============================================================
cat("Plotting Figure 2: Calibration...\n")

calib_models <- c("Poisson GLM","Hybrid GLM+GBM","GBM","XGBoost","Super Learner")

calib_df <- pred_df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(calib_models), names_to = "Model", values_to = "pred") %>%
  mutate(Model = factor(Model, levels = calib_models)) %>%
  group_by(Model) %>%
  mutate(decile = ntile(pred, 10)) %>%
  group_by(Model, decile) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_pred = mean(pred),
    avg_obs  = mean(y),
    .groups  = "drop"
  )

fig_calib <- ggplot(calib_df, aes(x = avg_pred, y = avg_obs, colour = Model)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 2.2) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = model_colors[calib_models]) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 0.001)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 0.001)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Calibration: Predicted vs Observed Claim Frequency",
    subtitle = "Policies grouped into deciles of predicted frequency (test set)",
    x        = "Average predicted frequency",
    y        = "Average observed frequency",
    colour   = "Model",
    caption  = "Points on the dashed line indicate perfect calibration. Each point represents one decile of predicted frequency."
  ) +
  thesis_theme

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "fig_calibration.png"), fig_calib,
       width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
cat("  Saved fig_calibration.png\n")



# ============================================================
# FIGURE 4 — DECILE LOSS RATIO CHART
# ============================================================
cat("Plotting Figure 4: Decile Chart...\n")

decile_list <- lapply(model_names, function(m) {
  pred_df %>%
    mutate(decile = ntile(.data[[m]], 10)) %>%
    group_by(decile) %>%
    summarise(
      avg_pred = mean(.data[[m]]),
      avg_obs  = mean(y),
      .groups  = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(Model = m)
})
decile_df <- bind_rows(decile_list) %>%
  mutate(Model = factor(Model, levels = model_names))

fig_decile <- ggplot(decile_df, aes(x = decile)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = avg_pred, colour = Model), linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(aes(y = avg_pred, colour = Model), size = 2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = avg_obs,  colour = Model), linewidth = 0.5, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_point(aes(y = avg_obs, colour = Model), size = 1.2, shape = 1) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = model_colors) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:10, labels = paste0("D", 1:10)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Predicted vs Observed Claim Frequency by Decile",
    subtitle = "Solid line = predicted | Dotted line = observed (same colour per model)",
    x        = "Decile of predicted frequency",
    y        = "Average claim frequency",
    colour   = "Model",
    caption  = "Models closer to the observed line across all deciles exhibit better calibrated risk discrimination."
  ) +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9))

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "fig_decile.png"), fig_decile,
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
cat("  Saved fig_decile.png\n")

cat("\n========== ALL FIGURES SAVED ==========\n")
cat("Location:", OUTPUT_DIR, "\n")
cat("  fig_residuals.png\n")
cat("  fig_calibration.png\n")
cat("  fig_lift.png\n")
cat("  fig_decile.png\n")

# ============================================================
# FIGURE 3 — CUMULATIVE LIFT CURVES (ranked by rate)
# ============================================================
cat("Plotting Figure 3: Lift Curves...\n")

# Rank on rate (predicted counts / exposure), evaluate on counts
pred_df_lift <- pred_df %>%
  mutate(
    `Poisson GLM`    = `Poisson GLM`    / exposure,
    GAM              = GAM              / exposure,
    GLMBoost         = GLMBoost         / exposure,
    `Hybrid GLM+GBM` = `Hybrid GLM+GBM` / exposure,
    GBM              = GBM              / exposure,
    XGBoost          = XGBoost          / exposure,
    `Super Learner`  = `Super Learner`  / exposure
  )

lift_df <- bind_rows(lapply(model_names, function(m) {
  d <- pred_df_lift[order(-pred_df_lift[[m]]), ]
  data.frame(
    Model      = m,
    pct_policy = seq_len(nrow(d)) / nrow(d),
    cum_lift   = cumsum(pred_df$y[order(-pred_df_lift[[m]])]) / sum(pred_df$y)
  )
})) %>%
  mutate(Model = factor(Model, levels = model_names)) %>%
  group_by(Model) %>%
  filter(row_number() %% 50 == 0 | row_number() == n()) %>%
  ungroup()

fig_lift <- ggplot(lift_df, aes(x = pct_policy, y = cum_lift, colour = Model)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = model_colors) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(
    title    = "Cumulative Lift Curves",
    subtitle = "Policies ranked by predicted claim rate (descending) — test set",
    x        = "Proportion of policies",
    y        = "Cumulative share of observed claims",
    colour   = "Model",
    caption  = "Higher and earlier separation from the diagonal indicates better risk discrimination."
  ) +
  thesis_theme

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "fig_lift.png"), fig_lift,
       width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
cat("  Saved fig_lift.png\n")
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# SUPER LEARNER COMPARISON GRAPHS — All 5 versions
# ============================================================

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)
library(ggrepel)   # for label repelling in scatter plot

# ============================================================
# BASE DATA
# ============================================================

sl_data <- data.frame(
  Model       = c("GLM", "GAM", "GBM", "XGBoost"),
  Individual  = c(42857.21, 42808.10, 40640.95, 40209.07),
  SL_Deviance = c(42859.67, 42584.55, 40614.69, 40360.14),
  SL_Weight   = round(sl_fit$coef[c("SL.glm.poisson_All","SL.gam.poisson_All",
                                     "SL.gbm.poisson_All","SL.xgb.poisson_All")] * 100, 1),
  RMSE        = c(0.2355, 0.2351, 0.2339, 0.2334),
  MAE         = c(0.09843, 0.09810, 0.09603, 0.09589),
  Gini        = c(0.1304, 0.1382, 0.1794, 0.1833)
) %>%
  mutate(
    Model = factor(Model, levels = c("GLM","GAM","GBM","XGBoost")),
    Ind_Rank = rank(Individual),
    Weight_Rank = rank(-SL_Weight)
  )

# ============================================================
# GRAPH 1 — SCATTER: Deviance vs SL Weight
# ============================================================
cat("Plotting Graph 1: Scatter...\n")

fig_scatter <- ggplot(sl_data, aes(x = Individual, y = SL_Weight,
                                    size = RMSE, colour = Model)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_text_repel(
    aes(label = paste0(Model, "\n(weight: ", SL_Weight, "%)")),
    size = 3.2, show.legend = FALSE, fontface = "italic",
    box.padding = 0.6, point.padding = 0.4
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    "GLM"     = "#7F7F7F",
    "GAM"     = "#AEC6CF",
    "GBM"     = "#E84B3A",
    "XGBoost" = "#2CA02C"
  )) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(4, 12), name = "RMSE") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = comma_format()) +
  labs(
    title    = "Does the Super Learner Reward the Best Individual Models?",
    subtitle = "X-axis: individual test deviance | Y-axis: stacking weight assigned by Super Learner\nPoint size proportional to RMSE",
    x        = "Individual Test Deviance (lower is better)",
    y        = "Super Learner Stacking Weight (%)",
    colour   = "Model",
    caption  = "GBM receives zero weight despite strong individual deviance, while GAM receives 35% weight.\nThis suggests the Super Learner selects for complementarity, not just individual performance."
  ) +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "fig_sl_scatter.png"), fig_scatter,
       width = 9, height = 6, dpi = 300)
cat("  Saved fig_sl_scatter.png\n")

# ============================================================
# GRAPH 2 — RADAR CHART
# ============================================================
cat("Plotting Graph 2: Radar Chart...\n")

# Normalise all metrics to 0-1 (1 = best)
radar_df <- sl_data %>%
  mutate(
    Dev_norm    = 1 - (Individual   - min(Individual))   / (max(Individual)   - min(Individual)),
    RMSE_norm   = 1 - (RMSE         - min(RMSE))         / (max(RMSE)         - min(RMSE)),
    MAE_norm    = 1 - (MAE          - min(MAE))          / (max(MAE)          - min(MAE)),
    Gini_norm   =     (Gini         - min(Gini))         / (max(Gini)         - min(Gini)),
    Weight_norm =     (SL_Weight    - min(SL_Weight))    / (max(SL_Weight)    - min(SL_Weight))
  ) %>%
  select(Model, Dev_norm, RMSE_norm, MAE_norm, Gini_norm, Weight_norm) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Model, names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(Metric = recode(Metric,
    "Dev_norm"    = "Deviance",
    "RMSE_norm"   = "RMSE",
    "MAE_norm"    = "MAE",
    "Gini_norm"   = "Gini",
    "Weight_norm" = "SL Weight"
  ))

# Close the polygon
radar_closed <- radar_df %>%
  group_by(Model) %>%
  group_modify(~ bind_rows(.x, .x[1, ])) %>%
  ungroup()

metrics      <- unique(radar_df$Metric)
n_metrics    <- length(metrics)
angles       <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = n_metrics + 1)[-(n_metrics + 1)]
angle_df     <- data.frame(Metric = metrics, angle = angles)

radar_plot_df <- radar_df %>%
  left_join(angle_df, by = "Metric") %>%
  mutate(
    x = Value * cos(angle),
    y = Value * sin(angle)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Model) %>%
  arrange(angle) %>%
  bind_rows(
    radar_df %>%
      left_join(angle_df, by = "Metric") %>%
      mutate(x = Value * cos(angle), y = Value * sin(angle)) %>%
      group_by(Model) %>%
      slice(1)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Grid circles
grid_df <- expand.grid(
  r      = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0),
  theta  = seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 100)
) %>%
  mutate(x = r * cos(theta), y = r * sin(theta))

# Axis lines
axis_df <- angle_df %>%
  mutate(x_end = cos(angle), y_end = sin(angle))

# Labels
label_df <- angle_df %>%
  mutate(
    x = 1.18 * cos(angle),
    y = 1.18 * sin(angle)
  )

fig_radar <- ggplot() +
  geom_path(data = grid_df, aes(x = x, y = y, group = r),
            colour = "grey85", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_segment(data = axis_df,
               aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = x_end, yend = y_end),
               colour = "grey80", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_polygon(data = radar_plot_df,
               aes(x = x, y = y, fill = Model, colour = Model),
               alpha = 0.15, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(data = radar_plot_df,
             aes(x = x, y = y, colour = Model), size = 2) +
  geom_text(data = label_df,
            aes(x = x, y = y, label = Metric),
            size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", colour = "grey30") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    "GLM"     = "#7F7F7F",
    "GAM"     = "#AEC6CF",
    "GBM"     = "#E84B3A",
    "XGBoost" = "#2CA02C"
  )) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "GLM"     = "#7F7F7F",
    "GAM"     = "#AEC6CF",
    "GBM"     = "#E84B3A",
    "XGBoost" = "#2CA02C"
  )) +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(
    title    = "Model Profile: Performance Metrics and Super Learner Weight",
    subtitle = "All metrics normalised to 0–1 (1 = best performance)",
    colour   = "Model", fill = "Model",
    caption  = "Deviance, RMSE, MAE: lower is better (inverted). Gini and SL Weight: higher is better."
  ) +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(
    axis.text  = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "fig_sl_radar.png"), fig_radar,
       width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 300)
cat("  Saved fig_sl_radar.png\n")

# ============================================================
# GRAPH 3 — SLOPE / BUMP CHART
# ============================================================
cat("Plotting Graph 3: Slope Chart...\n")

slope_df <- data.frame(
  Model        = c("GLM", "GAM", "GBM", "XGBoost"),
  Ind_Rank     = c(4, 3, 2, 1),   # rank by individual deviance (1 = best)
  Weight_Rank  = c(3, 2, 4, 1)    # rank by SL weight (1 = highest weight)
) %>%
  mutate(Model = factor(Model, levels = c("GLM","GAM","GBM","XGBoost"))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Ind_Rank, Weight_Rank),
               names_to = "Context", values_to = "Rank") %>%
  mutate(Context = recode(Context,
    "Ind_Rank"    = "Individual\nRanking",
    "Weight_Rank" = "Super Learner\nWeight Ranking"
  ))

fig_slope <- ggplot(slope_df, aes(x = Context, y = Rank,
                                   group = Model, colour = Model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.8, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_point(size = 5) +
  geom_text(
    data = slope_df %>% filter(Context == "Individual\nRanking"),
    aes(label = Model), hjust = 1.3, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = slope_df %>% filter(Context == "Super Learner\nWeight Ranking"),
    aes(label = paste0(Model, "\n(",
                       c("3.5%","35.0%","0.0%","61.6%")[as.numeric(Model)],
                       ")")),
    hjust = -0.15, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_y_reverse(breaks = 1:4, labels = c("1st","2nd","3rd","4th")) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    "GLM"     = "#7F7F7F",
    "GAM"     = "#AEC6CF",
    "GBM"     = "#E84B3A",
    "XGBoost" = "#2CA02C"
  )) +
  labs(
    title    = "How the Super Learner Re-ranks Individual Models",
    subtitle = "Left: ranking by individual test deviance | Right: ranking by Super Learner stacking weight",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Rank",
    caption  = "GBM drops from 2nd to last (zero weight). GAM rises from 3rd to 2nd.\nXGBoost remains dominant. This reflects complementarity, not just individual performance."
  ) +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    panel.grid      = element_blank()
  )

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "fig_sl_slope.png"), fig_slope,
       width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
cat("  Saved fig_sl_slope.png\n")

# ============================================================
# GRAPH 4 — DOT PLOT WITH DUAL METRICS
# ============================================================
cat("Plotting Graph 4: Dot Plot...\n")

dot_long <- sl_data %>%
  select(Model, Individual, SL_Deviance, SL_Weight) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Individual, SL_Deviance),
               names_to = "Context", values_to = "Deviance") %>%
  mutate(
    Context = recode(Context,
      "Individual"  = "Individually Fitted",
      "SL_Deviance" = "Evaluated in SL Framework"
    ),
    Model = factor(Model, levels = c("XGBoost","GBM","GAM","GLM"))
  )

fig_dot <- ggplot(dot_long, aes(x = Deviance, y = Model, colour = Context, shape = Context)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = Model), colour = "grey75", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_text(
    data = sl_data %>% mutate(Model = factor(Model, levels = c("XGBoost","GBM","GAM","GLM"))),
    aes(x = pmin(Individual, SL_Deviance) - 60,
        y = Model,
        label = paste0(SL_Weight, "%")),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 3.2, colour = "grey30", fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  annotate("text", x = min(sl_data$Individual) - 120,
           y = 4.45, label = "SL\nweight", size = 2.8,
           colour = "grey40", fontface = "italic") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    "Individually Fitted"        = "#E84B3A",
    "Evaluated in SL Framework"  = "#5B9BD5"
  )) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(
    "Individually Fitted"        = 16,
    "Evaluated in SL Framework"  = 17
  )) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = comma_format(), limits = c(39900, 43200)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Model Deviance: Individually Fitted vs Evaluated in Super Learner Framework",
    subtitle = "Each line connects the same model across two evaluation contexts.\nSL weight shown on the left.",
    x        = "Poisson Deviance (lower is better)",
    y        = NULL,
    colour   = NULL, shape = NULL,
    caption  = "Differences between contexts reflect different train/test splits. Relative ordering within each context is the key comparison."
  ) +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "fig_sl_dot.png"), fig_dot,
       width = 9, height = 5, dpi = 300)
cat("  Saved fig_sl_dot.png\n")

# ============================================================
# GRAPH 5 — HEATMAP
# ============================================================
cat("Plotting Graph 5: Heatmap...\n")

heat_df <- sl_data %>%
  mutate(
    Deviance_norm = 1 - (Individual - min(Individual)) / (max(Individual) - min(Individual)),
    RMSE_norm     = 1 - (RMSE - min(RMSE))             / (max(RMSE) - min(RMSE)),
    MAE_norm      = 1 - (MAE - min(MAE))               / (max(MAE) - min(MAE)),
    Gini_norm     =     (Gini - min(Gini))             / (max(Gini) - min(Gini)),
    Weight_norm   =     (SL_Weight - min(SL_Weight))   / (max(SL_Weight) - min(SL_Weight))
  ) %>%
  select(Model, Deviance_norm, RMSE_norm, MAE_norm, Gini_norm, Weight_norm) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Model, names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Score") %>%
  mutate(
    Metric = recode(Metric,
      "Deviance_norm" = "Deviance",
      "RMSE_norm"     = "RMSE",
      "MAE_norm"      = "MAE",
      "Gini_norm"     = "Gini",
      "Weight_norm"   = "SL Weight"
    ),
    Metric = factor(Metric, levels = c("Deviance","RMSE","MAE","Gini","SL Weight")),
    Model  = factor(Model,  levels = c("GLM","GAM","GBM","XGBoost")),
    Label  = round(case_when(
      Metric == "Deviance"  ~ sl_data$Individual[match(Model, sl_data$Model)],
      Metric == "RMSE"      ~ sl_data$RMSE[match(Model, sl_data$Model)],
      Metric == "MAE"       ~ sl_data$MAE[match(Model, sl_data$Model)],
      Metric == "Gini"      ~ sl_data$Gini[match(Model, sl_data$Model)],
      Metric == "SL Weight" ~ sl_data$SL_Weight[match(Model, sl_data$Model)]
    ), 4)
  )

fig_heat <- ggplot(heat_df, aes(x = Metric, y = Model, fill = Score)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Label), size = 3.2, colour = "grey20", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "#d73027",
    mid      = "#ffffbf",
    high     = "#1a9850",
    midpoint = 0.5,
    name     = "Relative\nScore"
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Full Performance Profile: Individual Metrics and Super Learner Weight",
    subtitle = "Colour shows relative performance (green = best, red = worst) within each metric.\nActual values shown inside cells.",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = NULL,
    caption  = "Deviance, RMSE, MAE: lower is better (colour inverted). Gini and SL Weight: higher is better."
  ) +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid      = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    axis.text.y     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11)
  )

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "fig_sl_heatmap.png"), fig_heat,
       width = 9, height = 5, dpi = 300)
cat("  Saved fig_sl_heatmap.png\n")

cat("\n========== ALL SL COMPARISON GRAPHS SAVED ==========\n")
```
